title: 10 best herbal capsules for dry mouth
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dry mouth are dietary supplements that help alleviate the symptoms of dry mouth by stimulating the production of saliva and maintaining moisture in the mouth. Examples of such herbal capsules include AYURVEDIC SYNERGY Sandalwood Soothe Drops and Nature's Bounty Slippery Elm. These capsules typically contain natural ingredients such as slippery elm, marshmallow root, and ginger, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help moisturize and protect the mouth's mucous membranes. By using herbal capsules for dry mouth, individuals can improve their quality of life by reducing discomfort, preventing tooth decay, and enhancing their ability to speak, swallow, and taste food properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden orach capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains a variety of beneficial compounds, including polyphenols and flavonoids, which have been shown to stimulate saliva production. This increased saliva flow can help alleviate the symptoms of dry mouth, such as difficulty speaking, swallowing, and chewing. Additionally, orach is rich in vitamins and minerals, including calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which can support overall oral health and help maintain a healthy balance of moisture in the mouth. By promoting saliva production and providing essential nutrients, orach capsules can be an effective natural remedy for dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Garden orach capsules have a mucilaginous quality that forms a soothing film over mucous membranes, providing relief from dry mouth symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are pectins and other polysaccharides found in the plant.
Anti-inflammatory: The plant contains various flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing irritation and swelling in the oral cavity caused by dry mouth.

Antioxidant: Garden orach is rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and carotenoids, which help protect oral tissues from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall oral health in dry mouth conditions.
Moisturizing: The plant's high water content and mucilaginous nature help increase saliva production and maintain hydration in the oral cavity, alleviating dry mouth symptoms.

Nutrient-dense: Garden orach is a good source of essential vitamins and minerals, including vitamin A, vitamin C, and calcium. These nutrients support the maintenance of healthy oral tissues, contributing to the overall management of dry mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the garden orach plant are rich in vitamins and minerals, making them ideal for making medicinal tea to alleviate dry mouth.
Stems: The stems of the garden orach plant contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help stimulate saliva production and reduce dry mouth symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the garden orach plant are a natural source of healthy oils and omega-3 fatty acids, which can help lubricate and moisturize the mouth, providing relief from dry mouth symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains compounds that stimulate the production of saliva. This herbal supplement has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help protect the mouth and throat from infections and inflammation. Dry mouth, also known as xerostomia, can be caused by various factors, including medication, radiation therapy, or medical conditions. By promoting saliva production, wild indigo capsule can alleviate the symptoms of dry mouth, such as difficulty speaking, swallowing, and tasting. Additionally, wild indigo has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can help reduce the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, further supporting oral health.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic property: Wild indigo capsules contain alkaloids that inhibit the parasympathetic nervous system, reducing saliva production and helping to alleviate dry mouth.

Anti-inflammatory property: The isoflavonoids present in wild indigo have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, providing relief from dry mouth symptoms.
Immunostimulant property: Wild indigo stimulates the immune system, helping the body to fight off infections and inflammation that can exacerbate dry mouth symptoms.
Analgesic property: Wild indigo contains compounds that have pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Astringent property: The tannins in wild indigo have astringent properties, which can help tighten and protect mucous membranes, providing temporary relief from dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild indigo are commonly used in making medicinal tea for dry mouth due to their ability to stimulate saliva production.
Leaves: The leaves of wild indigo are used in tea for their astringent properties, which can help relieve dry mouth symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of wild indigo contain antioxidants that can help protect the mouth and throat tissues from damage, making them useful in tea for dry mouth.
Seeds: The seeds of wild indigo are rich in mucilage, a substance that can help lubricate and moisturize the mouth, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for dry mouth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains high levels of echinacea, a natural ingredient known for its ability to stimulate saliva production. This herbal supplement has been traditionally used to treat various oral conditions, including dry mouth. By promoting saliva flow, echinacea helps to keep the mouth moist, reducing discomfort and preventing dental problems associated with dry mouth. Additionally, echinacea has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help to protect the mouth from infections and other complications. Overall, eastern purple coneflower capsule provides a natural and effective solution for managing dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is important for dry mouth as it can help alleviate irritation and discomfort in the mouth and throat. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides.

Immunostimulant: Echinacea purpurea can boost the immune system, which is crucial for dry mouth because a weakened immune system can exacerbate oral health issues. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are alkamides, glycoproteins, and polysaccharides.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Echinacea purpurea help protect oral tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for dry mouth as it can help maintain the health of oral tissues. The active constituents responsible for this property include cichoric acid, caftaric acid, and chlorogenic acid.

Analgesic: Eastern purple coneflower has pain-relieving properties, which is important for dry mouth as it can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with dryness, irritation, or sores in the mouth. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkamides and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties that can help maintain a healthy oral microbiome, which is important for dry mouth as it can help prevent infections and further complications. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkamides, essential oils, and polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, the flowers help stimulate saliva production to alleviate dry mouth.
Leaves: Containing high levels of tannins and flavonoids, the leaves can soothe irritation and moisturize the mouth.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients and mucilage, the seeds create a protective film over the mouth's mucous membranes, providing relief from dry mouth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains adaptogenic compounds that support the body's ability to cope with physical and environmental stress. One of the ways it does this is by stimulating the salivary glands, which increases the production of saliva in the mouth. This helps to alleviate the symptoms of dry mouth, such as difficulty speaking, swallowing, and chewing. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce any inflammation in the salivary glands, further promoting saliva flow. Furthermore, it also helps to improve overall oral health by reducing the risk of gum disease and tooth decay, which can be caused by dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic: Siberian ginseng helps the body adapt to stress and fatigue, which can reduce the severity of dry mouth by promoting overall well-being and balance. The active constituents responsible for this property are eleutherosides and polysaccharides.

Immune system support: Siberian ginseng boosts the immune system, which can help prevent infections and inflammation in the mouth, contributing to dry mouth. The active constituents responsible for this property are eleutherosides and glycosides.
Increased saliva production: Siberian ginseng stimulates saliva flow, helping to alleviate dry mouth symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are saponins and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Siberian ginseng has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, alleviating dry mouth symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are eleutherosides and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Siberian ginseng has antioxidant properties, which can help protect cells in the mouth from damage, promoting overall oral health and reducing dry mouth symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are eleutherosides and polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Siberian ginseng are commonly used to make medicinal tea for dry mouth due to their ability to stimulate saliva production.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Siberian ginseng are rich in saponins, which can help relieve symptoms of dry mouth by increasing the moisture in the mouth.
Leaves: The leaves of Siberian ginseng contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritation in the mouth caused by dryness.
Seeds: The seeds of Siberian ginseng contain essential oils that can help stimulate the production of saliva, providing relief from dry mouth.
Bark: The bark of Siberian ginseng contains glycosides, which can help promote the production of saliva, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal tea for dry mouth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains active compounds that stimulate the production of saliva. The main ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to increase the volume and flow of saliva, thereby alleviating the symptoms of dry mouth. This herbal supplement may also help to soothe irritation and inflammation in the mouth and throat, providing additional relief for those suffering from dry mouth. Furthermore, licorice capsules can help to protect the oral mucosa and prevent the growth of harmful bacteria, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice root contains compounds that form a sticky film over mucous membranes, providing relief from dry mouth by keeping the tissues moist. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and other polysaccharides.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms. The triterpenoid compounds, including glycyrrhizin, are responsible for this property.
Saliva stimulation: Licorice can help increase saliva production, providing further relief for dry mouth. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Licorice has antimicrobial properties that can help maintain a healthy oral environment, preventing infections that could worsen dry mouth symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Soothing effects: Licorice has soothing properties that can help alleviate irritation and discomfort caused by dry mouth. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice capsule plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that can stimulate the production of saliva, helping to alleviate dry mouth.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice capsule plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritation in the mouth.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice capsule plant contain essential oils that can help moisturize the mouth and provide relief from dryness.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice capsule plant contain antioxidants that can help protect the mouth from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guar capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains a natural gum called guar gum, which is derived from the guar bean. This gum has the ability to absorb and hold water, making it an effective saliva substitute. When taken, guar gum swells up and forms a gel-like substance, which coats the mouth and throat, providing immediate relief from dryness. Additionally, guar gum stimulates the salivary glands to produce more saliva, helping to maintain moisture in the mouth over time. This dual action of providing instant hydration and promoting saliva production makes guar capsule a useful remedy for managing dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Guar capsules contain guar gum, a natural polysaccharide that can help moisturize the mouth by increasing saliva production.
Lubrication: The demulcent properties of guar gum in guar capsules can provide a soothing, lubricating effect on the mucous membranes, reducing discomfort caused by dry mouth.
Saliva stimulation: Certain compounds in guar gum can stimulate salivary glands, promoting saliva secretion and alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
pH balancing: Guar capsules can help maintain a healthy oral pH level, which is important for dry mouth sufferers as an imbalanced pH can exacerbate dry mouth symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory: Guar gum has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, providing relief for those experiencing dry mouth-related irritations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which can stimulate saliva production and help alleviate dry mouth.
Seeds: Contain mucilaginous substances that can lubricate and moisten the mouth, providing relief from dry mouth symptoms.
Bark: Has demulcent properties due to its polysaccharide content, forming a protective film over mucous membranes and helping to soothe dry mouth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains compounds that can increase saliva production. The herb has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat dry cough and respiratory conditions, and recent studies suggest that it may also help alleviate dry mouth symptoms. English ivy extract can stimulate the salivary glands, thereby increasing saliva flow and providing relief from dry mouth. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties may help protect the mouth and throat from infections and irritations, further contributing to its effectiveness in managing dry mouth.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic property: English ivy capsules can help reduce dry mouth symptoms by decreasing saliva production through anticholinergic effects, mainly due to the presence of Hederasaponin B and Hederagenin.

Demulcent property: English ivy has a soothing effect on mucous membranes, providing relief for dry mouth by forming a protective film over the oral tissues, thanks to its mucilaginous polysaccharides.

Anti-inflammatory property: English ivy exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate dry mouth-related irritation and inflammation in the oral cavity, attributed to the flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids present in the plant.
Expectorant property: English ivy can help stimulate saliva production and clear phlegm, easing dry mouth symptoms, due to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Antimicrobial property: English ivy possesses antimicrobial properties that can help maintain a healthy oral environment and prevent dry mouth-associated infections, owing to its diverse phytochemicals, including saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy capsule plant contain high levels of saponins, which can help stimulate saliva production and alleviate dry mouth symptoms.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain mucilaginous compounds that can help soothe and moisturize the mouth and throat, providing relief from dry mouth.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy capsule plant have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat and alleviate dry mouth.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant contain flavonoids and other antioxidants that can help protect the mouth and throat from damage caused by dry mouth.
Seeds: The seeds of the English ivy capsule plant contain essential fatty acids and other nutrients that can help nourish and moisturize the mouth and throat, providing relief from dry mouth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort capsule helps with dry mouth because it contains active compounds that stimulate the production of saliva.
